rationale: The original det.rs was missing all type dependencies (ID, KeyTrait, VerusClone, DelegationMap) and a verus!{} wrapper, so it failed to parse. Rebuilt the file as a self-contained Verus module mirroring the sibling 'host_model_next_get_request__get' template: defined AbstractEndPoint (with valid_physical_address), an ID struct whose view returns its ep field, stub KeyTrait/VerusClone traits, and a DelegationMap<K> whose view exposes a Map<K, AbstractEndPoint>. Inside the LLM PROOF BLOCK, asserted r1.ep == r1@ and r2.ep == r2@ and the chaining r1@ == self_@[k] && r2@ == self_@[k] ==> r1.ep == r2.ep; since ID is a single-field struct, structural equality on ep gives r1 == r2 (det_get_equal), discharging the postcondition under both the g_neq_tuple-true and false cases. verus exited 0 (1 verified, 0 errors).
